Disable FEATURE_PGO on WebAssembly, iOS, TVOS, and MacCatalyst (#126493)

`FEATURE_PGO` (PGO infrastructure) is not meaningful on WebAssembly,
iOS, TVOS, or MacCatalyst targets and should be excluded from those
builds. `FEATURE_PGO` is now gated under the

fix: repair redirect tester + add GET /api/links/{link_id} endpoint (#19)

## Summary

Fixes the **Redirect tester** feature in the UI, which was silently
broken due to a browser security restriction.

## Root cause

The frontend `testRedirect()` function used `fetch()` with `redirect:
'manual'`. For cross-origin requests (frontend on `:5173`, backend on
`:8000`) this produces an **opaque redirect** response — the browser
hides all headers including `Location`, and `res.status` is always `0`.
As a result, the tester always showed blank status and location.

## Changes

### Backend
- Added `GET /api/links/{link_id}` endpoint that returns `LinkOut`
metadata without triggering a redirect — a clean REST resource read

### Frontend
- `testRedirect()` now calls `GET /api/links/{link_id}` and displays
**redirect code**, **target URL**, and **active** status
- Added `getLink()` helper to `api.ts`

### Tests
- Two new integration tests for the new endpoint (200 with metadata, 404
for unknown ID)
- All 30 tests pass, linting clean

## Also on this branch
- `docs: remove rate limiting requirement` — minor docs update

* fix: skip cache.saveCache() on Windows to work around @actions/glob bug (actions/toolkit#2085)

Co-authored-by: carlosperate <4189262+carlosperate@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: provide require polyfill in ESM bundle so minimatch resolves path.sep correctly on Windows

The root cause of the Windows cache bug (actions/toolkit#2085) is that
minimatch v3 uses `require('path')` with a try/catch fallback to
`{ sep: '/' }`. When bundled as ESM by rollup, `require` is not
available, so the fallback activates and `path.sep` becomes '/' on
Windows. This disables minimatch's built-in backslash-to-forward-slash
conversion in its match() method, causing @actions/glob to never match
any paths.

The fix adds `createRequire` from Node's `module` builtin as a rollup
banner/intro, making `require` available in the ESM bundle. This allows
minimatch's `require('path')` to resolve correctly, enabling proper
Windows path handling without patching any dependencies.

Also reverts the "skip saveCache on Windows" workaround so caching
works on all platforms.

commit.message:
docs(spec): correct Vec<Rc<T>> rejection claim to method-call-site enforcement (#888)

The previous wording (from PR #886) stated that Vec<Rc<T>> is
'rejected by the type checker' alongside HashMap and HashSet, implying
annotation-level enforcement for all three.  In reality:

- HashMap<Rc<T>, V>, HashMap<K, Rc<T>>, and HashSet<Rc<T>> ARE rejected
  at the annotation level in validate_vec_element_type / key-type checks.
- Vec<Rc<T>> is NOT banned at annotation level; the rejection is
  enforced per-method in hew-types/src/check/methods.rs at the call
  sites for push, pop, get, remove, set, append, extend, map, filter,
  and fold.

Update both occurrences (§3.7.5 Rc/Arc and the Vec<T> element
restrictions paragraph) to reflect this distinction accurately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
sec: Fix ~60 SQL injection vulnerabilities across 15 files (#1469)

* Initial plan

* fix: parameterize SQL queries in reportonbilledphcp.jsp to prevent SQL injection

Replace 24 vulnerable SQL string concatenations with parameterized queries
using DBPreparedHandler.queryResults(sql, String[]) overload:

- 2 standalone queries (diagnostic_code list, service_code list) now use
  new String[]{startDate, endDate} parameter arrays
- 22 in-loop queries use sqlParams arrays with appropriate parameters:
  - bDx branches: 4 params (vServiceCode, providerNo, startDate, endDate)
  - non-bDx branches: 5 params (startDate, endDate, providerNo, vServiceCode, vServiceDesc)
- 2 constant-SQL queries (provider list, dxphcpgroup) left unchanged (safe)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SQL injection vulnerabilities in 4 files

- ExtractBean.java (MSP): Validate invNo as numeric via Integer.parseInt()
  before concatenation in dbExtract query (dbExtract lacks PreparedStatement)

- FrmDischargeSummaryRecord.java: Convert GetSQL() with concatenated
  values to GetPreSQL() with parameterized queries for casemgmt_issue
  and issue table lookups

- MSPReconcile.java (ca.bc): Convert 4 methods from GetSQL() with
  string concatenation to GetPreSQL() with parameterized queries:
  - getBillsByType: demographic lookup with b.demoNo
  - getMSPRemittanceQuery: s21Id and payeeNo parameters
  - getAdjustmentsMSPRemittanceQuery: s21Id and payeeNo parameters
  - hasMatchingBillingRecord: billingmasterNo, dataCenterNo, receivedDate

- RptFormQuery.java: Add SQL identifier pattern validation for table
  names retrieved from reportConfig before concatenation into SQL.
  Uses regex pattern to ensure only valid identifier characters.

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback

- ExtractBean.java: Improve comment explaining dbExtract limitation
  (Statement-only, no PreparedStatement) and add TODO for refactoring
- RptFormQuery.java: Remove potentially malicious table name from
  SecurityException message to avoid leaking attack patterns; log
  error separately via MiscUtils.getLogger()

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve SQL injection vulnerabilities in EFormUtil

- getEFormGroups: parameterize demographic_no via DBHandler.GetPreSQL()
- listEForms: parameterize group via PreparedStatement, validate sortBy
  against allowed constants
- listPatientEForms (deprecated overload): parameterize demographic_no
  and groupName, validate sortBy against allowed constants
- Add ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS set and validateSortBy() helper to whitelist
  ORDER BY values (cannot be parameterized with ?)
- Use LogSanitizer in validation error messages to prevent log injection

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SQL injection in report data classes: parameterize all queries

Convert all string-concatenated SQL queries to use parameterized queries
via DBHelp.searchDBRecord(sql, params...) in three report data classes:

RptReportItem.java:
- getReportName(): reportId concatenated into WHERE clause
- getNameList(): status int concatenated (hardened for consistency)

RptReportConfigData.java:
- insertRecordWithOrder(): report_id concatenated into WHERE clause
- getConfigFieldName(): reportId and saveAs concatenated
- getConfigNameList(): reportId and saveAs concatenated
- getConfigObj(): reportId and saveAs concatenated
- getReportTableNameList(): reportId concatenated

RptReportFilter.java:
- getNameList(int): status concatenated (hardened for consistency)
- getNameList(String, int): recordId and status concatenated

All String reportId parameters are now parsed via Integer.parseInt()
before binding, which both validates the input as numeric and passes
the correct type to the PreparedStatement.

Resolves SQL injection alerts in:
- reportFormConfig.jsp (4 alerts)
- reportResult.jsp (3 alerts)
- reportFilter.jsp (2 alerts)
- reportFormDemoConfig.jsp (2 alerts)
- reportFormOrder.jsp (2 alerts)
- reportFormCaption.jsp (1 alert)

Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix SQL injection vulnerabilities in PatientSearch.jsp and logReport.jsp

Add therapy analysis and fix AID basal assessment bug

- New tools/report/therapy_analyzer.py: deep therapy settings analysis
  accounting for AID loop compensation. Uses per-night overnight drift
  instead of misleading metabolic flux, IOB circadian profiling,
  correction bolus ISF estimation, hypo safety analysis, weekly
  trend decomposition, and evidence-based recommendations.

- Fix assess_basal() AID bug: metabolic net_flux shortcut gave wrong
  answer for AID patients because flux includes loop adjustments
  (temp basals, SMBs), not just programmed basal. Overnight glucose
  slope is the reliable signal. Patient with flat overnight glucose
  (-0.03 mg/dL/hr) was incorrectly assessed as 'too_high' due to
  large negative net_flux from Loop's automated insulin delivery.

- Enhanced HTML report (generate_html_report.py): new Therapy tab with
  IOB circadian chart, weekly TIR stacked bars, overnight drift bars,
  hypo by hour histogram, dawn phenomenon analysis, and therapy
  recommendations panel with priority-colored cards.

- Added 4 tests for basal assessment including regression test for
  AID flux override bug (89 tests total, all passing).

Therapy findings for live-recent patient:
  - Basal: mixed (51% rising, 39% falling) → loop compensating
  - ISF: profile 40, effective 44 (1.10×) → reasonable
  - Only 11 manual boluses / 6 carb entries in 97 days
  - 1.0 hypo/day (17 serious) → safety concern
  - Weekly TIR range 25-90% → variable (external factors)
  - Dawn phenomenon: IOB 0.5U→2.1U (1-3AM to 4-7AM)

Split x64/arm64 builds into parallel pipeline stages (#14497)

* Split x64/arm64 builds into parallel pipeline stages

Restructure the CI/CD pipeline to build x64 and arm64 in parallel
instead of sequentially, reducing end-to-end build time.

Pipeline shapes:
- PR: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → test (uses installer.msix directly)
- Nightly: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → package → test (dev-cert bundle)
- Release: build_x64 ∥ build_arm64 → package → test (ESRP-signed bundle)

Key changes:
- Extract shared build-job.yml template parameterized by platform
- Add package-stage.yml that creates msixbundle from both platform
  artifacts, ESRP-signs for release, dev-cert signs for nightly
- PR tests run immediately after x64 build using installer.msix
  (no package stage, no bundle needed)
- Release/nightly tests wait for the package stage and test the real
  signed bundle that gets published
- CloudTest configs are parameterized: release tests pull the bundle
  from the [package] artifact, PR tests use installer.msix from [drop]
- arm64 + formatting checks always run in parallel with x64 but don't
  block the PR test gate
- CodeQL runs in the arm64 stage (off the critical path)
- flight-stage and nuget-stage updated for new stage names

* formatting

* Remove redundant runtime conditions on release-only tasks

These tasks are already wrapped in compile-time conditionals which
prevent them from being added to the pipeline definition for
non-release builds. The runtime condition checks can never evaluate
to false at that point, so they are pure noise.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback from OneBlue

- Remove wslcsdk NuGet staging (wslc is not in master)
- Always include [package] provider in TestMap.xml.in instead of
  conditionally injecting via PACKAGE_PROVIDER_BLOCK cmake variable.
  PR builds simply use [drop] as TEST_PACKAGE_PROVIDER; the [package]
  provider exists but is unused.
- Add BUNDLE_ONLY cmake option so the package pipeline stage reuses
  cmake's existing bundle target instead of forking makeappx logic.
  This locks the SDK version via cmake (CMAKE_SYSTEM_VERSION) and
  keeps bundle creation logic in one place (msixinstaller/CMakeLists.txt).
  The pipeline now copies msix files to expected paths, runs a fast
  cmake configure with -DBUNDLE_ONLY=TRUE, and builds the bundle target.
- Remove dead NuGet binary restore step in package stage.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Fix PACKAGE_VERSION regex: escape dots so only A.B.C.D is accepted
- Consolidate CMake defaults (build type, config types, output dir)
  before BUNDLE_ONLY block to avoid duplication
- Nightly tests now use the full bundle from the package stage instead
  of installer.msix (new INCLUDE_PACKAGE_STAGE cmake variable)
- Package stage reuses version output from build stage instead of
  recomputing it

Co-authored-by: joelp172 <51311693+joelp172@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: improve rate limiter robustness, error reporting, and test reliability

Add stderr warnings when UPTIME_ROBOT_RATE_LIMIT, UPTIME_ROBOT_MAX_RETRIES,
and UPTIME_ROBOT_BASE_DELAY env vars are set to invalid values instead of
silently falling back to defaults. Hash API keys in the global limiter
registry to avoid holding secrets as plaintext map keys. Simplify
getSharedLimiter to a single atomic LoadOrStore call.

Harden tests with resetGlobalLimiters cleanup, wider timing tolerances for
CI, boundary value subtests (zero, negative, float), and a nil limiter
guard test. Update comments and docs to clarify burst=rate relationship.

* fix(e2e): match JSON-formatted controller-runtime log output in metrics test

The metrics endpoint test asserts that controller logs contain
'controller-runtime.metrics\tServing metrics server' (tab-delimited text
format), but the controller outputs JSON structured logs where this appears
as 'msg': 'Serving metrics server'. The substring never matches, causing a
120s timeout that cascades into the webhook test failing because AfterAll
deletes the namespace before the next test suite runs.

Accept both text and JSON log formats using SatisfyAny.

* refactor: parse env vars once via sync.Once, use full hash, add Load fast path

Address review comments:
- Wrap env var parsing in sync.Once so warnings for invalid values are
  emitted once at startup instead of on every per-reconcile NewClient call.
- Use the full SHA-256 digest in limiterKey instead of truncating to 8
  bytes, eliminating collision risk between different API keys.
- Re-add Load fast path in getSharedLimiter before LoadOrStore to avoid
  allocating a rate.Limiter on every NewClient call when one already exists.
- Add resetClientConfig test helper and call it in subtests so each test
  gets a fresh sync.Once for env var parsing.

* fix: read UPTIME_ROBOT_API on every NewClient call instead of caching

The sync.Once refactor inadvertently cached UPTIME_ROBOT_API alongside the
numeric/duration env vars. Controller tests change this env var between
reconciles to simulate API failures, so caching it caused 6 test failures
where expected errors became nil. Keep UPTIME_ROBOT_API read per-call while
only caching the parsed numeric settings that benefit from sync.Once.

* fix(e2e): reset e2eInfraReady after Manager AfterAll tears down infrastructure

The Manager test suite's AfterAll undeploys the operator and deletes the
namespace, but never resets e2eInfraReady. The webhook test suite's
BeforeAll calls ensureE2EInfra() which returns immediately because the
flag is still true, then fails creating resources in the deleted namespace.

* fix: remove codeql workflow

* fix: close resp.Body in rate limiter tests to prevent resource leaks

commit.message:
[#305] Implement Gazebo robot MCP server (#325)

## Summary

Implement a robot-focused FastMCP server for the Dr.QP Gazebo
environment. This adds host-side and devcontainer-side control paths for
booting and shutting down the robot, reading robot and world state, and
recording robot state snapshots over time.

## Changes

### Features
- Add a new `drqp_robot_mcp` Python package that exposes a FastMCP
server for robot lifecycle and simulation inspection workflows.
- Add MCP tools to boot the robot to `torque_on`, shut it down through
the lifecycle state machine, read the latest robot state, record state
snapshots, and fetch Gazebo world entity poses.
- Register the new MCP server in the workspace MCP configuration so it
can be launched from the repository virtual environment.

### Configuration
- Add package metadata and console entrypoint so the MCP server can be
installed and run from the existing Python environment.
- Add the new package to the repository requirements so editable
installs include the MCP server.

### Tests
- Add unit coverage for the host-side controller lifecycle and recording
behavior.
- Add unit coverage for parsing Gazebo pose output into structured
world-state data.

### Documentation
- Add package README covering installation, startup, and the supported
MCP capabilities.

## Technical Details

### Approach
- The implementation is split into a host-side controller and a
devcontainer-side bridge.
- The host-side controller is responsible for starting the devcontainer
service, invoking bridge commands, coordinating lifecycle transitions,
and managing in-memory robot state recording.
- The bridge runs inside the devcontainer so ROS 2 and Gazebo operations
can execute in the environment where those tools are available.
- Robot state is assembled from ROS topics, while world state is
assembled from Gazebo pose output plus the ROS clock.

### Dependencies
- Add `mcp[cli]>=1.26.0` for the FastMCP server and CLI runtime.

### Assumptions
1. The existing devcontainer compose service is the correct execution
environment for ROS 2 and Gazebo operations.
2. The robot lifecycle can be driven through the existing `/robot_event`
and `/robot_state` topics.
3. Running the MCP server from the repository virtual environment is the
intended local workflow.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- Added controller tests covering boot-up, shutdown, and state recording
flows.
- Added parser tests covering Gazebo pose message extraction.

### Integration and CI
- PR checks currently show successful docs, validation, reformat, and
Codecov runs.
- An additional `Agent` check is still in progress.

### Manual Testing
- Not documented in the branch changes.
- I have not run this PR branch locally from the current workspace
session because the local checkout is on a different branch.

## Breaking Changes

No breaking changes expected.

## Risk Assessment

### Risks
- The MCP server depends on Docker Compose, the devcontainer service,
and ROS/Gazebo tooling being available and healthy at runtime.
- Lifecycle transitions assume the current robot state machine topics
and event names remain stable.

### Mitigation
- The controller raises explicit errors when container commands fail or
when expected state transitions do not occur.
- Unit tests cover the controller flow and the Gazebo pose parsing logic
to reduce regression risk in the core orchestration code.

## Related

- Closes #305

## Checklist

- [x] Code follows project conventions
- [x] Tests added for new logic
- [x] Documentation updated
- [x] No breaking changes expected
- [ ] Manual runtime verification completed against a live Gazebo
session

---------

commit.message:
ci: split monolithic CI/CD pipeline into three workflows (#26)

Split the single Build/Test/Release workflow (ci.yml) into three
separate, focused workflows:

1. ci.yml (Build/Test) -- runs on PRs, pushes to main, and manual
   dispatch. Handles validation, linting, testing, security scanning,
   image builds, plugin builds, and multi-arch manifests. Images are
   pushed to GHCR only on push to main. The version job uses git
   describe instead of bump logic. The release job and all release-
   related inputs (bump, draft, prerelease, dry_run) are removed.

2. prepare-release.yml (Prepare Release) -- manual workflow_dispatch
   with a bump level input (patch/minor/major). Computes the next
   semver from the latest tag, creates a release/v{version} branch,
   inserts the version heading into CHANGELOG.md, and opens a PR
   to main.

3. release.yml (Release) -- triggers via workflow_run after Build/Test
   completes on main. Checks if the merge came from a release/v*
   branch; if so, extracts the version from CHANGELOG.md, creates
   and pushes the git tag, re-tags per-arch images with the release
   version using imagetools, re-renders Kubernetes manifests with
   proper image references, downloads plugin binaries from the CI
   run, generates and signs an SBOM, and creates a draft GitHub
   release.

This separation provides clearer responsibility boundaries: CI runs
on every code change, release preparation is an explicit human-
triggered step, and the actual release is fully automated after the
release PR merges.
